output: Implemented batch 7 / T7 by adding the requested focused test coverage for canonical social publication storage, publish service behavior, caller-path routing, scheduler lifecycle, and publication_id-based delete handling. I also added a small `tests/conftest.py` harness to stub missing local integrations (`tweepy`, LLM package surface) and constrain async tests to the installed asyncio backend, plus a minimal Python 3.8 typing compatibility fix in `src/common/db_handler.py` so the real import path could load under the local runtime. Verification passed with the required throwaway smoke script run-and-delete flow and a final full `python -m pytest tests/ -v` run showing 15/15 passing.
commands_run: rg -n "list\[[^]]+\]" src/common/db_handler.py src/features/sharing src/features/admin_chat src/features/reacting main.py tests scripts | head -n 100
deviations: Added `tests/conftest.py` beyond the five requested test modules so the suite could run against the real code path in this local environment without installing missing optional dependencies or Trio.

Made a small incidental fix in `src/common/db_handler.py` to replace Python 3.9+/3.10-only typing syntax with Python 3.8-compatible annotations; without that change the new tests could not import the production modules under the local runtime.

task_updates.executor_notes: Added focused tests covering canonical social publication CRUD and atomic-claim RPC flow, route fallback, service publish/enqueue/delete behavior, caller-specific defaults (`text_only` for admin replies and no dedupe for reaction bridge), scheduler lifecycle through `cog_load` plus claim/execute/retry flow, and publication_id-based delete with explicit retweet rejection and multiple-publication disambiguation. Verified with the required throwaway smoke script (created, ran successfully, then deleted) and a final full `python -m pytest tests/ -v` rerun passing 15/15 tests.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed the new tests cover (1) atomic claim semantics via the DB helper RPC path, (2) caller-specific defaults including admin reply `text_only=True` and reaction bridge no-dedupe behavior, (3) scheduler lifecycle through `cog_load` plus claim/execute/retry flow, (4) publication_id-based delete with clear retweet rejection, and (5) multiple publications for the same Discord message. Final full-suite verification passed with `python -m pytest tests/ -v` showing 15/15 passing.
